problem.summary: Capture a fixed-buffer inference step into a HIP Graph and compare ordinary launches, capture/instantiate cost, and steady-state replay latency.
inputs: preallocated input, output, and workspace buffers
one or more kernels or library calls eligible for capture
outputs: graph executable and replay timing record
constraints: Template-only; no launch-overhead or inference speedup claim is attached
Shapes, addresses, workspace sizes, and stream dependencies must remain stable during graph replay
Record whether timing includes graph capture, instantiation, parameter update, or replay only

verification.method: Run ordinary-launch and graph-replay paths on identical fixed buffers and compare outputs before collecting timing.

curation.notes: ROCm parity port from CUDA task `graph-captured-inference-step`. This is a template/harness scaffold only; CUDA-origin result artifacts were intentionally not copied, and no ROCm timing, correctness, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed yet. Run on AMD hardware, attach ROCm version, hipcc flags, gfx target, and label HIP-event-only results as timing-only.
